name: alert
title: Alert
description: Static callout for important, often time-sensitive information. Five state variants with a per-variant icon (plus optional heading) for non-color redundancy, a uniform border, slim layout, opt-in live-region semantics, and an accessible 44px dismiss button.
useWhen: Summarizing form validation errors above a form
Confirming a completed action on the same page (success)
Warning about consequences before a user proceeds
Publishing an emergency or urgent service notice
Announcing an async result by injecting an alert with the live prop
avoidWhen: Field-level validation messages (use the Field error slot)
Transient notifications that disappear on their own (use a toast)
Blocking decisions that need a response (use a dialog)
Marketing or promotional callouts (alerts are for status, not persuasion)

keyboard: The alert body is static content and never takes focus
When dismissible, Tab moves focus to the Dismiss button (a native button with an accessible name)
Enter or Space activates Dismiss, which calls onDismiss; the consumer removes the node
After removing a dismissed alert that held focus, the consumer must move focus to a stable nearby element so it does not fall back to <body>
With live set, role=status (polite) or role=alert (assertive) is announced by screen readers without any keyboard interaction
